pois.short_description: Family-owned and operated USA Boxing registered gym offering training for all ages and fitness levels.

The gym values community, family, and a high-quality training experience, welcoming individuals into an extension of their home.
pois.origin_story: The Alvarez family opened the gym, realizing a longtime dream despite the pandemic.

pois.short_description: Brazilian and Hispanic-inspired cafe serving breakfast, sides, smoothies, and desserts in Salem, MA.
pois.long_description: Camilla's Cafe & Breakfast is a cafe offering an array of delicious breakfast options, infused with the passion for Brazilian and Hispanic flavors. The cafe is a beloved gathering spot for locals and visitors alike, known for its warm hospitality and diverse cultural influences.
pois.origin_story: In 2001, Graziela and Ney, a Brazilian couple, moved to the United States and honed their coffee shop skills. In 2011, they opened “Bread of Dreams” in Lynn, and in 2022, they expanded with “Camilla’s Cafe & Breakfast” in Salem.

pois.short_description: Family owned and operated bakery and coffee shop serving cakes, pastries, and breakfast to the North Shore for over 30 years.
pois.long_description: We are a family owned and operated bakery and coffee shop proudly serving delicious cakes and pastry to the North Shore for over 30 years. Our categories include cakes, specialty beverages, pastries, breakfasts, and more! We are kind of “Old School” but we keep evolving towards more scratch made bakery foods, great breakfast sandwiches, delicious cakes and interesting beverages.
pois.origin_story: In 1978, the owners purchased the “Donut Ring” shops in Salem and Peabody. In 1988, the Bridge Street store was renamed “Coffee Time Bake Shop” and began evolving from a donut shop to a bakery by adding “Real Cream Bismarks” and expanding into cakes.

pois.short_description: Modern metaphysical lifestyle brand and shop in Salem offering witchy and handmade products from independent makers.
pois.long_description: HausWitch Home + Healing is a modern metaphysical lifestyle brand and shop, providing Salem locals and visitors with a selection of witchy and handmade products from independent makers from around New England and the US. HausWitch combines the principles of earth magic, meditation, herbalism, and interior decorating to bring magic and healing into everyday spaces. HausWitch will always be an inclusive space for all genders, sexualities, ethnicities, abilities, and anyone who feels like they are in need of a truly supportive and safe environment in this ever-changing world.

pois.origin_story: Lifebridge North Shore began in 1978 when The Crombie Street Congregational Church committed to providing outreach to the homeless population, starting as a soup kitchen serving meals two evenings a week.

They offer square-pattied hamburgers, chicken sandwiches, fresh-made salads, hot and crispy fries, chili, and baked potatoes. They also have a bakery with sweet treats and Kids’ Meals.

pois.short_description: An innovative public school serving students in grades 4-8, fostering a community that empowers children to become capable, self-determining individuals.
pois.long_description: Marblehead Community Charter Public School has been an innovative school serving students in grades 4-8 since 1995. The school is designed to support each student’s maximum individual growth in order to continue successfully on their educational path, in particular preparing them for high school and beyond. MCCPS creates an environment that promotes community and project-based / service learning, culminating in a trimester Exhibition where students share their learning with the community.
pois.origin_story: Established in 1995.

pois.short_description: Non-profit tennis club in Salem, MA, offering indoor and outdoor courts, lessons, camps, and racquet services.
pois.long_description: Northshore Tennis is a non-profit tennis club serving the Boston area and North Shore. It offers 2 indoor tru-bounce surfaced courts, 2 outdoor Har-Tru courts, locker rooms, racquet repairs & stringing, youth and adult lessons, summer camps, and pickleball. The facilities are named the John L. Foley Tennis Center in honor of the club's retired tennis pro and New England Tennis Hall of Famer.
pois.origin_story: The club was founded over 55 years ago and the facilities were renamed the John L. Foley Tennis Center in honor of John Foley, who served as Head Tennis Pro since the club opened. Harold Zimman was the driving force behind the construction of the club.

pois.short_description: A mission-driven social justice art program featuring large-scale murals and educational tours.
pois.long_description: The Punto Urban Art Museum is a mission-driven social justice art program created by North Shore CDC, a community development non-profit. They believe public art can integrate neighborhoods and create resilient communities. The museum contains 75 large-scale murals featuring 40 global and 25 New England-based artists, and offers educational tours on social justice, equity, and segregated neighborhoods.
pois.origin_story: Created by North Shore CDC, a community development non-profit founded in the neighborhood in 1978.

pois.long_description: PZA Grille is committed to serving a better pizza – made from the finest fresh ingredients. They also serve fresh sandwiches, subs, burgers and more! The restaurant offers a comfortable, modern setting and takeout options.

New restaurant in the Salem Waterfront Hotel & Suite serving breakfast, lunch, cocktails and dinner every day offering a classic and creative menu.

pois.display_name: Angela's Preschool & Daycare Salem HS, LLC
pois.short_description: Childcare and early education center providing programs for infants, toddlers, and preschoolers.
pois.long_description: Angela's Preschool & Daycare offers both childcare and early education, fostering an environment where every child is treated like family and every day is an opportunity for growth, learning, and love. They provide a safe, enriching environment where little ones can explore, develop, and thrive under the care of compassionate, experienced professionals.
pois.origin_story: Founded with a deep sense of passion and inspired by Angela Hiraldo, the mother of founder Kelly Hiraldo, who dedicated her life to nurturing and supporting young minds. Her warmth, wisdom, and unwavering dedication to children shaped the foundation of the center.

pois.short_description: PEM’s Korean collection reflects Korea and Korean life from the late Joseon to the present, and is one of the most important in the United States.
A historic library and cultural center in Salem, Massachusetts, offering a collection of over 50,000 volumes and a variety of cultural programs.
pois.long_description: The Salem Athenæum is a historic library and cultural center with roots dating back to 1760. Originally formed from the merger of the Social Library and the Salem Philosophical Library in 1810, it has served as a hub for learning and community engagement for over two centuries. Today, the Athenæum offers a circulating and research collection of over 50,000 volumes, a welcoming reading room, and a garden, hosting concerts, readings, lectures, performances, and social gatherings.
pois.origin_story: The Salem Athenæum’s history begins with the founding of the Social Library in 1760 and the Salem Philosophical Library in 1781. These two institutions merged in 1810 to create the Salem Athenæum.

pois.short_description: Salem Public Library is located in an 1855 renovated brick mansion originally owned by sea merchant John Bertram.
pois.long_description: The Salem Public Library is located in the McIntire Historic District of Salem, Massachusetts, in an 1855 renovated brick mansion originally owned by sea merchant John Bertram. It is part of the North of Boston Library Exchange (NOBLE), a consortium of 25 public and academic libraries. The library provides quality public service in a reliable, caring, and cost-effective manner.
pois.origin_story: The building was originally built as a home for Captain John Bertram and his family in 1855. He was a successful merchant and ship owner who rose from poverty to wealth through shrewd investments and a willingness to take calculated risks. He was known for his generosity and founded numerous charities in Salem.

pois.short_description: Local Salem pizza shop offering subs, calzones, and pizza with fresh ingredients.
pois.long_description: Salem House of Pizza is a local staple in Salem, offering delicious subs, calzones, and pizza. They pride themselves on using fresh ingredients and providing excellent service to all customers.

pois.short_description: Psychic offering channeled readings on love, family, and career, as well as mediumship and seances.
pois.long_description: The Salem Love Psychic provides channeled readings from Spirit to answer questions on love, family, and career. They also offer mediumship to contact those from beyond, with private readings and group seances available. Readings are conducted in person with a cup of tea, or via phone and video conferencing.

pois.short_description: Bloodcurdling museum in Salem, Massachusetts, showcasing authentic instruments of torture and life-size displays of cruel punishments.
pois.long_description: The Salem Museum Of Torture is dedicated to the education and preservation of historical methods of criminal punishment. The museum is home to a collection of authentic instruments of torture accompanied by life-size displays of cruel & unusual punishments. By confronting this haunting legacy, the museum fosters a deeper understanding of our collective past and strives for a more compassionate, enlightened future.

Our veterinarians and staff are committed to promoting responsible pet ownership and preventive health care with a full-service medical facility offering general services like routine vaccinations, microchipping, dental and surgical care and more.

pois.short_description: A Salem lounge celebrating the bygone neighborhood bar with a welcoming atmosphere, sincere service, affordable food and drink, and a vintage jukebox.
pois.long_description: All Souls is a Salem lounge created in 2021 by two locals who share an affinity for the community and industry. It aims to celebrate and pay homage to the bygone neighborhood bar—an honest-to-goodness haunt where everybody knows your name. The lounge focuses on old-school hospitality: a welcoming atmosphere, sincere service, and affordable food and drink. It offers a vintage jukebox, classic cocktails, and a massive chili dog.

pois.short_description: HVAC and plumbing company serving the North Shore area, including Salem and Marblehead, offering repair, installation, and maintenance services.
pois.long_description: Townsend Energy, formerly Bartlett & Steadman, is a full-service HVAC and plumbing company serving the North Shore area for over 60 years. They provide honest assistance, high-quality work, and compassionate customer service for both residential and commercial clients. Services include new construction, remodels, repairs, installations, and routine maintenance, with a focus on safety, compliance, and customer satisfaction.

The restaurant features a variety of fine dishes for any palate, including fresh seafood, Black Angus steaks, refreshing salads, and gourmet flat breads, complemented by a wide selection of choice beer and wine. They host special occasions and functions, and can accommodate up to 240 guests during warmer months with their outdoor café.

pois.short_description: The Phillips Library at the Peabody Essex Museum inspires learning with its vast collection of manuscripts, books, maps, and ships’ logbooks.
pois.long_description: PEM’s Phillips Library, among the oldest libraries in the United States, inspires new journeys of learning. Heir to the remarkably rich holdings of PEM’s ancestor institutions, the Phillips Library provides vital support to curious minds at the museum, in our local and regional communities and across the globe. The library’s holdings reflect PEM’s collections of art in all media and across all curatorial departments in addition to its own long-standing strengths.
pois.origin_story: A library has been at the core of the institution since PEM’s founding in 1801. Today’s Phillips Library is the result of the integration of the James Duncan Phillips Library of the Essex Institute with the Stephen Phillips Library of the Peabody Museum of Salem in 1992, when their parent organizations merged.

pois.long_description: Gillian's Foods is committed to providing the highest quality products for individuals with allergies or intolerances. pois.short_description: Salem’s award-winning food and cultural walking tours offering delicious tastings at local shops and restaurants.
pois.long_description: Since 2012, Salem’s award-winning food and cultural walking tours. Enjoy delicious tastings at local shops and restaurants. Learn about Salem’s illustrious spice trade history, as well as modern day Salem’s thriving modern culinary scene. Tours run year-round.

They are members of the largest retailer-owned hardware cooperative in the industry, ensuring a wide selection of ACE products and personalized customer service. In 2013, the Houle family also purchased Manchester Hardware, expanding their reach.
pois.origin_story: Winer Bros. Paint & Hardware has been a family-owned business since 1989, with owner Allan Houle dedicated to providing the best service in the region. The Houle family expanded in 2013 by purchasing Manchester Hardware.

They specialize in repair, replacement, and installation of plumbing, heating, and boiler systems, including warm air furnaces, steam boilers, and hot water boilers. They also offer gas pipe installation, fixture replacement, and emergency services.
pois.origin_story: For the last decade, Essex County, MA has relied on Blaisdell Plumbing and Heating. The owner, Kyle Blaisdell, named the company after himself and strives to deliver old-world craftsmanship.

They provide website design, SEO optimization, e-commerce solutions, and video production, committed to providing 100% satisfaction through every phase of the design project.

pois.short_description: Museum offering historical presentations on the 1692 witch trials and the evolving image of the witch.
pois.long_description: The Salem Witch Museum, founded in 1972, offers two historical presentations. The first tells the tragic story of the 1692 witch trials and takes place in a large auditorium with life-sized stage sets which are dramatically narrated to immerse visitors in the world of 17th-century Salem. The second exhibit, Witches: Evolving Perceptions, examines the European witchcraft trials, the evolving image of the witch, and the larger issues of persecution and scapegoating in American history.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1972.

They aim to maximize eligible deductions and protect clients from costly mistakes, helping individuals and small businesses preserve their income.

pois.short_description: Plaque marking the approximate location of the Salem Witch Trials courtroom in 1692.
pois.long_description: The Bewitched statue depicts actress Elizabeth Montgomery astride a broom and framed by a moon crescent. It's located in Lappin Park and has attracted many visitors since its dedication on June 15, 2005. Some locals initially opposed the statue, but business owners welcomed it as a tourist attraction.
pois.origin_story: The statue was donated by TV Land and dedicated in 2005, inspired by the four episodes of the show 'Bewitched' set in Salem.

pois.short_description: Hands-on DIY workshops, private events and craft kits for all ages to spark creativity and fun.
pois.long_description: Create & Escape is a studio filled with color, creativity, and community, offering hands-on DIY workshops, private events and craft kits for all ages. They aim to provide a place where creativity feels effortless, projects turn out amazing, and crafting is fun, not frustrating. They offer step-by-step guidance, high-quality materials, and trendy projects.
pois.origin_story: Debbie & Wendy, a sister duo, founded Create & Escape with the idea of creating a place to connect, unwind, and celebrate through creativity.

pois.short_description: Salem distillery crafting exceptional spirits with reverence for tradition and a touch of irreverence, offering tours and tastings.
pois.long_description: Damn Righteous Spirits is a distillery in Salem dedicated to crafting exceptional spirits. Their creative process blends reverence for tradition with a playful irreverence, resulting in spirits found at the intersection of art and science. They offer tours and tastings with the founders, providing an in-depth look at the distilling process and a complimentary tasting.
pois.origin_story: The distillery draws inspiration from a historical event in Salem during the 1830s involving Reverend George Cheever and distiller John Stone, aiming to revive the art of distilling in a town with a rich spirit-making history.

pois.short_description: Salem Pediatric Dental & Orthodontic Associates provides comprehensive preventive and therapeutic oral health care for infants, children, and adolescents.
pois.long_description: Salem Pediatric Dental & Orthodontic Associates is committed to delivering oral health care in a simple, enjoyable, and educational way. They offer the latest advances in pediatric dental care and orthodontic treatment for patients of all ages, with a focus on early intervention and preventative care. Pediatric dentists have completed specialized training to care for children, and the practice welcomes patients with special health care needs.
pois.origin_story: Dr. Stuart Gould founded Salem Pediatric Dental in 1972 following years of specialized training.

pois.short_description: H&H Propeller specializes in the customization, fabrication, and repair of marine driveline components for recreational, commercial, and government vessels.

They serve recreational boaters, marinas, and shipyards, providing services ranging from optimization and maintenance to large-scale commercial repairs. The company reopened in recent years under new ownership, retaining experienced craftsmen and expanding its workshop capabilities to handle boats of all sizes.
pois.origin_story: H&H Propeller was originally established with a legacy of quality work, and recently reopened as a new company under new ownership, retaining over ten craftsmen from the original team since 1950.

pois.short_description: Historic Salem, Inc. champions the architectural preservation and protection of Salem, Massachusetts since 1944.
pois.long_description: As one of America’s oldest civic historic preservation groups, Historic Salem, Inc. has championed the architectural preservation and protection of Salem, Massachusetts since our founding in 1944. We have brought together residents, experts, and advocates to safeguard Salem’s historic places, working collaboratively to ensure that change respects our city’s past while supporting a vibrant future.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1944, Historic Salem, Inc. has championed the protection of Salem’s historic architecture for over 80 years.

pois.short_description: Salem’s Wicked Walking Tour for children and families, offering a kids’ eye view of Salem’s history.
pois.long_description: Salem Kids Tours offers a walking tour for children and families, allowing kids to discover history, mystery, and a little magic. The tour provides a kids’ eye view of Salem’s captivating past, with true historical tales, interactive storytelling, and kid-appropriate content. All tour guides are certified Massachusetts educators.
pois.origin_story: Alicia Diozzi, a middle school history teacher, created Salem’s only kid-focused walking tours in 2013.

pois.short_description: Upscale, luxurious Victorian guest rooms in a c1843 building featuring wide pine floors, exposed beams, and antique furniture.
Counseling center offering trauma-informed care for children, adolescents, and adults, focusing on mind, body, and spirit connection.
pois.long_description: Celestial Healing Counseling Center LLC provides compassionate, trauma-informed care for children, adolescents, and adults, offering individual and family therapy in a grounding and supportive space. The center honors the connection between mind, body, and spirit, supporting healing that is both evidence-based and deeply human. They offer a holistic approach to therapy, valuing collaboration, consistency, and meeting clients where they are.
pois.origin_story: Becca Chernek founded Celestial Healing Counseling Center to offer compassionate, trauma-informed care that honors the connection between mind, body, and spirit.

pois.short_description: Store offering shipping, packaging, printing & other services, plus office supplies.
Pizza company offering hand-stretched, fresh dough, unique toppings, and craft beers in a sci-fi decorated space.
pois.long_description: Flying Saucer Pizza Company is a Salem restaurant offering a quirky and delicious pizza experience. They specialize in gourmet pizzas inspired by fresh and local ingredients, paired with a draught beer selection featuring beers from the Northeast. The restaurant has a “nerd”vana vibe, decorated with Doctor Who, Star Trek, and Star Wars memorabilia.

pois.short_description: Salem diner serving fresh, hot breakfast and brunch with a bohemian vibe.
pois.long_description: The Ugly Mug Diner is a go-to spot for fresh, hot breakfast and brunch served with love in historic Salem, Massachusetts. They offer a variety of fan favorites like Chipotle Brisket Hash, Mugly’s Chicken & Waffle, and incredible Benedicts, along with mimosas, bloody marys, sandwiches, and a rotating lineup of specials.

pois.short_description: Episcopal church in Salem, Massachusetts offering services and bible study.
Hair salon in Salem offering customized color and haircut services with low-tox, cruelty-free, and vegan-friendly products.
pois.long_description: Moon Baby Hair Salon is a collective of creative and intuitive hairstylists, and a place where their craft and specialties intertwine with self-expression, sustainability and support through hair. Their mission is to offer clients a warmhearted and dreamy space to express themselves through hair, while extending guidance and building trust. They offer customized and craft hair color and haircut services, ranging from classic to current.

pois.short_description: U.S.-based refiner, marketer, and transporter of quality fuels and lubricants with a history spanning over 110 years.
pois.long_description: CITGO Petroleum Corporation is a U.S.-based refiner, marketer, and transporter of quality fuels, lubricants, petrochemicals, and other petroleum-based industrial products. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, CITGO operates three highly sophisticated crude refineries with a combined capacity of 829,000 barrels per day and owns a large network of terminals, pipelines, and blending plants. The company markets its products through independently owned retail outlets and directly to various industries.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1910 by Henry L. Doherty, initially as the Cities Service Company, with a vision of creating exceptional business opportunities and improving the lives of everyday people. The company evolved and rebranded as CITGO in 1965.

pois.short_description: Bohemian cafe and craft beer bar in downtown Salem serving coffee, light food, crepes, and hard-to-find craft beer since 2005.
pois.long_description: Gulu Gulu Cafe is a bohemian outpost offering hard-to-come-by craft beer, coffee, and light food. This popular coffeehouse teems with a motley mix of artists and young professionals, hosts local bands and singer-songwriters, and features a focus on visual and performance art. The food leans wholesome and veggie-friendly with soups, sandwiches, and an expansive crepe menu.
pois.origin_story: Marie Feldmannova and her husband Steve Feldmann named their quirky place for the cafe in Prague where they met. They opened their first Gulu Gulu Cafe in Lynn in 2005, and the Salem location opened in 2007.

pois.short_description: Membership program for the Peabody Essex Museum offering exclusive access, benefits, and engagement opportunities.
pois.long_description: East India Marine Associates (EIMA) are invited to participate in exclusive programs that bring them closer to the heart of the Peabody Essex Museum. Memberships support the museum and provide benefits such as unlimited free admission, discounts at the Museum Shop and Atrium Café, reciprocal admission to 1,500+ museums, curator-led tours, invitations to exhibition openings, and opportunities to join Visiting Committees or attend exclusive events.

pois.short_description: H&H Propeller specializes in the customization, fabrication, and repair of marine driveline components for recreational, commercial, and government vessels.
pois.long_description: H&H Propeller is a marine driveline specialist offering customization, fabrication, and repair services for recreational, commercial, and government vessels. They recently reopened under new ownership, retaining experienced craftsmen from its legacy since 1950, and expanded their workshop with greater machining capabilities. They recondition propellers and shafts of all materials and sizes, and fabricate new shafts up to eight inches in diameter and 40 feet in length. Their technicians are experts in analysis and computer-aided optimization, ensuring optimal performance and exceeding original equipment specifications.
pois.origin_story: H&H Propeller was recently reopened as a new company under new ownership, but retained over ten craftsmen who helped build the company’s legacy of quality work since 1950.

pois.short_description: State-of-the-art dental office in Salem, MA providing quality, comfortable, and affordable dental care with a focus on preventive care.
pois.long_description: Coral Dental Care is a state-of-the-art dental office dedicated to providing quality, comfortable, and affordable dental care. The staff stays abreast of the latest dental technologies and procedures to ensure exceptional care. They prioritize preventive care and infection control, offering thorough examinations, x-rays, routine checkups, and cleanings to prevent dental decay and disease. The office is designed to provide the finest treatment and best service, equipped with digital x-rays and intra-oral cameras.

pois.short_description: Worldwide provider of corporate and professional supplies, including notary supplies, corporate kits, and personalized awards, based in Salem, Massachusetts.
pois.long_description: The Corporate Connection is a worldwide provider of corporate and professional supplies. They offer a wide range of products including engraved plates, laser engraved seal dies, sublimation flash printed stamps, gold lettering stock books, signs, name plates, notary supplies, engineer/architecture seals and stamps, corporate kits, rubber stamps, embossers, name badges, employee awards, stock certificates, and plant markers.
pois.origin_story: The business was started in 1989 by a husband and wife team looking to increase their service to notaries.

pois.short_description: Neighborhood bar and restaurant in Salem, MA, serving flatbread pizzas and offering shuffleboard, darts, and arcade games.
pois.long_description: Longboards is a lively, casual space perfect for locals and visitors. The bar originated from a small variety store called The Hungry Whale, which the Mattera brothers transformed into a popular sandwich shop specializing in lobster rolls in 2011. In 2013, they expanded into a dining room and full bar, and in September 2023, they moved to a larger location while retaining the original building across the courtyard. Longboards is known for its unique flatbread pizzas served on long boards.
pois.origin_story: Brothers Justin and Matthew Mattera, North Shore natives, started with a small variety store called The Hungry Whale in 2011, which they turned into a sandwich shop. In 2013, they expanded into a dining room and full bar. They moved to a larger space in September 2023.

pois.short_description: Regional planning agency for the 101 cities and towns of Greater Boston, established in 1963.
pois.long_description: MAPC is the regional planning agency for the 101 cities and towns of Greater Boston. We work to create a more sustainable, equitable, collaborative, and climate-resilient region. MAPC helps communities uplift their unique histories and heritage, offering expertise in historic preservation and public humanities alongside cultural vitality, tourism, and economic development.
pois.origin_story: Established in 1963, MAPC is a public agency created under Massachusetts General Law Chapter 40B Section 24.

pois.short_description: Salem Orthodox church offering Divine Liturgy on Sunday mornings, rooted in the teachings of Holy Scripture and the Early Church.
pois.long_description: St. Nicholas Orthodox Church is committed to placing Jesus Christ first in daily life and growing in holiness through repentance, forgiveness, and prayer. The church offers a full experience of worship, appealing to all the senses, and welcomes children and visitors alike. They host a coffee hour after Sunday services and offer Christian education for all ages.
pois.origin_story: St. Nicholas parish was founded in Salem in 1901 by immigrants from Galicia and became part of the Russian Orthodox Church in 1908. In 1970, it joined the newly established Orthodox Church in America (OCA).

pois.short_description: Independent insurance agency serving Peabody and the North Shore for over 25 years, offering tailored insurance packages for home, auto, and business.
pois.long_description: Peabody Insurance Agency is a local, family-owned business that has been servicing the Peabody and North Shore residents for over 25 years. Their mission is to provide clients with superior service tailored to meet their insurance needs, recognizing that each customer has different requirements. They offer customized packages for home, commercial, auto, and other coverage, and their friendly staff is trained to answer questions, offer advice, and solve problems.
pois.origin_story: Peabody Insurance Agency has been servicing the Peabody and North Shore residents for over 25 years.

pois.short_description: Salem, MA-based food manufacturer specializing in gluten-free, wheat-free, dairy-free, tree nut-free, and peanut-free products including rolls, bread, pasta, pizza, and desserts.
pois.long_description: Gillian’s Foods is dedicated to providing foods you love without the harmful ingredients you must avoid. They offer a wide range of gluten-free, wheat-free, dairy-free, tree nut-free, and peanut-free products, including rolls, bread, pasta, pizza, and desserts, all made in a dedicated facility.
pois.street_address: 45 Congress St. Bld. 4, Suite 160, Salem, MA 01970

pois.display_name: Shrine of St. Nicholas the Wonderworker
pois.short_description: A house of prayer, charity, social entrepreneurship education, and encounter, with plans to host Marblehead Brewing Co. and Marblehead Salt Co.
pois.long_description: St. Nicholas is a place of encounter which shortens distances and enriches with differences. As the headquarters of St. Paul’s Foundation, it launched Ryan’s Guitars Project to protect the innocence of children in Eastern Europe and the Middle East. It is an educational laboratory to teach people of faith economic development and social entrepreneurship. Once completed, St. Nicholas will be the home of the Marblehead Brewing Co. and the Marblehead Salt Co. It is also a place of prayer, offering services twice a day in the Orthodox Christian tradition, and a space for private contemplation with a chapel, icons, and a rose garden.

pois.short_description: Salem's premiere escape room destination offering immersive stories, challenging puzzles, and satisfying solves for all experience levels.
pois.long_description: Stained Hourglass Escapes is a team of artists, engineers, and performers dedicated to creating unforgettable experiences. They design and build everything in-house, prioritizing the player experience. They aim for immersive stories, challenging puzzles, and satisfying solves for all experience levels, and have become known as the premiere escape room destination in Salem.

pois.short_description: Creative Collective is a 350+ member business support ecosystem serving entrepreneurs, makers, small businesses, nonprofits, and creative professionals across Essex County, Massachusetts.
pois.long_description: Creative Collective exists because not every business fits the traditional mold. They support the whole person, not just the business, through a collaborative community where members learn from each other. They deliver real business services through a community where members learn from each other, including marketing, consulting, technical assistance, advocacy, and event production.
pois.origin_story: Creative Collective was founded to support businesses that don't fit the traditional mold, such as vintage shop owners, digital artists, and food truck entrepreneurs.

pois.short_description: Free, charter public school in Salem serving grades 6-12 with a college preparatory program and community service focus.
pois.long_description: Salem Academy Charter School is a small 6th – 12th grade public school designed to educate the City of Salem’s diverse student population. Through a unique integration of college preparatory classes with service to the community, the school graduates informed, articulate and proactive individuals of strong character. The school community is based on mutual respect and thoughtful relationships, and students are encouraged to pursue school norms defined as REACH – Responsible, Empathetic, Assertive, Cooperative, Honest.
pois.origin_story: Salem Academy Charter School opened its doors to sixth and seventh graders in September 2004 with the promise to serve the diverse population of Salem.

pois.short_description: Museum offering self-guided walking tours exploring Salem's historic homes and properties.
pois.long_description: The Peabody Essex Museum (PEM) offers PEM Walks, a self-guided walking tour that explores the stories of Salem's historic homes and properties. The city is known worldwide for the Salem Witch Trials of 1692 and boasts a rich cultural heritage.
pois.origin_story: PEM has an origin story like no other.

pois.street_address: 90 Lafayette St Suite 1, Salem, MA 01970, USA

pois.short_description: Full-service commercial print shop and design studio serving businesses and organizations across the North Shore of Massachusetts and the Greater Boston area.
Pet supply and service retailer offering grooming, training, adoptions, and veterinary care.

pois.display_name: A. Lichelle Aldana, DDS, PC Family Dentistry
pois.street_address: 6 Colby St Unit B, Salem, MA 01970, USA

pois.short_description: Jewelry designer creating custom and handcrafted fine jewelry in Salem, Massachusetts.
pois.long_description: Jenni Stuart designs and creates exclusive, handcrafted fine jewelry in her in-house studio. Every piece is made with care and attention to detail by expert goldsmiths, offering high-quality craftsmanship without the typical markups. Specializing in new heirlooms and bespoke designs, Jenni Stuart works directly with clients to create unique and personalized pieces.

pois.short_description: A 200-year-old Chinese home dismantled and reconstructed at the Peabody Essex Museum, offering a glimpse into the life of the Huang family.
pois.long_description: Yin Yu Tang is a stately 16-bedroom house originally built in China’s southeastern Huizhou region during the Qing Dynasty. It was home to the Huang family for over 200 years before being carefully dismantled and transported to Massachusetts as part of a cultural exchange. Re-erected at PEM, the house tells the story of the Huang family and offers insight into historical changes in China and their impact on daily life.
pois.origin_story: The house was built by a prosperous merchant named Huang in the Qing Dynasty (1644–1911) and was home to eight generations of the Huang family before being dismantled in the 1990s and reconstructed at PEM.

pois.short_description: Salem shop offering gifts, souvenirs, and spiritual tools with a focus on witchcraft and metaphysical items.
pois.long_description: Moons Gift Shop is a haven for those seeking a touch of the otherworldly and enchantment. Curated by Karen Davis and Sarah Loiacono, the shop offers an assortment of witchy items, metaphysical wonders, and handcrafted treasures that resonate with the essence of Salem’s magick. Visitors can explore Salem souvenirs infused with a touch of the supernatural. Moons Gift Shop is a sacred space for the mystic and magical, encouraging self-discovery and offering a portal to a world where the ordinary and extraordinary dance in harmony.
pois.origin_story: Karen Davis previously operated Coon’s Card and Gift Shop for 22 years before reimagining it as Moons Gift Shop, representing her journey to her higher self as 'Wonder Witch'.

pois.short_description: A community of museum enthusiasts, artists and collectors connected to the Peabody Essex Museum.
pois.long_description: GenPEM is a community of museum enthusiasts, artists and collectors connected to the Peabody Essex Museum. It provides access to innovative programming, special curator-led tours and pop-up events around Boston and the North Shore. Members gain invitations and complimentary tickets to events, guest passes, parking vouchers, and reciprocal admission to other museums.

pois.short_description: The Salem Area Chamber of Commerce connects businesses to a powerful community of leaders and advocates for regional growth.
pois.long_description: The Salem Area Chamber of Commerce is dedicated to helping businesses achieve their goals through advocacy, involvement, and visibility. Membership connects businesses to a network of over 1,000 businesses and provides a voice in improving the region’s business climate.

pois.short_description: Salem dental practice offering comprehensive dental care with a focus on patient comfort and infection control.
pois.long_description: Dr. Aldana's office is committed to providing the finest personal service and treatment, prioritizing patient care and comfort. They offer a relaxing atmosphere and a team dedicated to educating patients and fulfilling their dental needs. The practice emphasizes infection control and utilizes advanced sterilization techniques.
pois.origin_story: Celebrating over 29 years of making Salem smile.

pois.short_description: Restaurant in Salem, MA offering fresh, flavorful breakfast and lunch.
pois.long_description: Salem's Retreat is a restaurant founded out of a love for real food, focusing on fresh, healthy, and authentic ingredients. They strive to create wholesome dishes and offer a variety of options, from classic breakfast fare to specialty sandwiches and salads.
pois.origin_story: Salem's Retreat opened in 2020, but its history is linked to a British raid on Salem in 1775, where local militia forced a retreat after a swift response. The area is known for being the site of the first blood shed in the American Revolution.

pois.short_description: Locally owned cannabis dispensary in Salem offering a welcoming atmosphere and knowledgeable staff.
Martial arts school offering Taekwondo training for kids, adults, and families, focusing on self-confidence, self-esteem, and physical/mental development.
pois.long_description: DW Kim's US Taekwondo Center has become a landmark of martial arts in Beverly, Lynn, and Middleton. Led by Grand Master Do Wun Kim, the center offers a modern Taekwondo curriculum and training, emphasizing a positive, safe, and injury-free environment. The program focuses on self-improvement, discipline, and perseverance, helping students build physical strength, mental focus, and character.

pois.short_description: Well-appointed guest rooms with private baths in downtown Salem.

pois.display_name: Black Veil Shoppe of Drear & Wonder
pois.short_description: Shop offering a variety of grim artists, clothing designers, antiques, oddities, jewelry, and crafted goods.

pois.display_name: Count Orlok's Nightmare Gallery Monster Museum
pois.short_description: Museum displaying wax figures of vampires, zombies & other characters from horror & monster films.
pois.long_description: Count Orlok's Nightmare Gallery is a monster museum offering a collection of monsters and mayhem. It also features a horror gift shop with merchandise available online.

pois.short_description: EB Skin Studio offers a wide range of skincare services including facials, chemical peels, and waxing, tailored to each client's unique needs.
pois.long_description: At EB Skin Studio, we specialize in skincare and offer a wide range of services from consultations and custom facials to chemical peels, dermaplaning, and LED light therapy. We also offer Korean Skincare Services, utilizing a holistic approach to skin health. In addition to facials, we provide waxing, threading, lash and brow services, and retail skincare products to help maintain healthy, glowing skin between appointments.

pois.short_description: Salem’s only open-air rooftop bar and restaurant serving strong drinks and simple food with views of Salem’s harbor.
pois.long_description: The Roof offers Salem’s only open-air rooftop bar and restaurant, serving strong drinks and simple food. Located at the top of The Hotel Salem, this seasonal dining space features an outdoor kitchen and expansive views of Salem’s cobblestone streets and bustling harbor. It provides a laid-back, yet sophisticated atmosphere for locals and tourists alike.

pois.short_description: Salem paddle tour offering kayak and paddle board lessons, rentals, and tours along the historic coastline.
pois.long_description: Discover a different side of Salem with our Salem Paddle Tour! Beyond its famous Witch Trials, Salem boasts a stunning oceanfront perfect for exploration. Glide along its historic coastline, once the world’s richest port, and immerse yourself in breathtaking natural beauty and maritime heritage. Ideal for beginners and seasoned paddlers alike, our tours offer a unique adventure through Salem’s sheltered coves under the guidance of passionate experts.

pois.short_description: Progressive religious community in Marblehead offering worship services, religious exploration, and social justice initiatives.
pois.long_description: The Unitarian Universalist Church of Marblehead is a liberal religious community that celebrates spiritual and intellectual inquiry and growth. They are inspired by Unitarian Universalist values and principles, practicing acceptance, compassion, and love within the church and the wider world. The church offers worship services, lifespan religious exploration programs, and opportunities for social justice and action.
pois.origin_story: Founded as the Second Congregational Church in 1716, the church quickly adopted Unitarianism and Universalism, playing a significant role in the lives of notable individuals like Rev. Edward Holyoke, who became President of Harvard College, and General John Glover.

pois.short_description: Upbeat American Tavern in downtown Salem offering a vibrant atmosphere, classic American fare, and upscale comfort food.
pois.long_description: The Village is an upbeat American Tavern located in the heart of downtown Salem, offering guests a vibrant atmosphere and a memorable dining experience. With comfortable plush seating, a spacious patio, and three full-service bars, we are proud to serve classic American fare and upscale comfort food that brings family and friends together. The Village is a social center where friends reconnect, share stories, and build lasting relationships.

pois.short_description: Indoor cycling studio with locations in Beverly, Amesbury, North Reading, and Salem, MA, offering a welcoming community for riders of all levels.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 2011, Inner Cycle Studio began as one of the first dedicated indoor cycling studios on the North Shore and has evolved into a community of riders of all ages and fitness levels.
pois.street_address: 142 Canal St Suit B, Salem, MA 01970

pois.short_description: To Do Ta Done provides personal assistance, organization, and home management services to help clients reclaim their time and manage busy lives.
pois.long_description: To Do Ta Done began as a personal errand service and has grown into a full-service operation handling routine assistance, large-scale projects, remodels, property staging, and home management. The team includes professional Personal Assistants and Organizers, supported by experienced movers, contractors, and real estate professionals. They aim to provide a stress-free experience for clients navigating life's challenges and transitions.
pois.origin_story: Mona Hersey founded To Do Ta Done when her youngest child started preschool, seeking a way to earn income while maintaining flexibility. She identified a need for reliable assistance with daily tasks and created a service to help others juggle work, family, and endless to-do lists.

pois.short_description: A friendly, loving church family in downtown Salem offering Sunday worship, coffee fellowship, and a focus on prayer and spiritual gifts.
pois.long_description: Wesley United Methodist Church is a welcoming congregation seeking to exalt Jesus Christ and make disciples. They emphasize prayer, the power of the Holy Spirit, and allowing individuals to share their spiritual gifts. The church offers traditional hymns, contemporary praise music, scripture readings, and a sermon during Sunday worship, followed by coffee and fellowship.
pois.origin_story: The congregation began with circuit rider Jessie Lee preaching in Salem in the early 1800s, meeting in a storefront before purchasing a building on Harbor Street. The current building on North Street was dedicated in 1888 and originally known as Wesley Episcopal Church. Lafayette St UMC merged with Wesley UMC in 1994.

pois.short_description: Salem shop carrying magic tricks, novelties, costumes, and occult items such as books, candles, and statues.
Comic shop in downtown Salem, Massachusetts, offering new and back issue comics, original art, Pop Culture products, and horror collectibles.
pois.long_description: Silver Moon Comics and Collectibles is a comic shop located in downtown Salem, Massachusetts, across from the Peabody Essex Museum. They offer a wide range of products including comic book back issues, original art, Pop Culture products, Golden Age to current Comics, hard to find Horror collectibles, trade paperbacks, and film and television memorabilia. They also offer a subscription service with discounts on comics and trade paperbacks.
pois.street_address: 1 East India Square Mall #131, Salem, MA 01970, USA

pois.short_description: The Peabody Essex Museum (PEM) in Salem, MA, features accredited arboretum campus gardens and green spaces, including the Museum Garden and Ropes Mansion Garden.
pois.long_description: PEM’s campus is an accredited arboretum and offers numerous gardens and green spaces for exploration. The Museum Garden, designed by Nelson Byrd Woltz Landscape Architects, is a 5,000-square-foot award-winning garden reflecting the era of Asian maritime trades. The Ropes Mansion Garden, designed in 1912, is a one-acre Colonial Revival garden open to the public 365 days a year. The museum’s gardens and green spaces cover 4.1 acres in Salem, MA, and an additional 5.3-acre campus in Rowley, MA.

pois.short_description: Premier destination for exquisite wands crafted for aspiring witches and wizards.
Historic shop selling exquisite teas, soaps, scented candles, and antiques.
Boutique in downtown Salem offering clothing, accessories, and jewelry.
Nonprofit community arts organization offering locally created fine art, crafts, jewelry, and gifts.
pois.long_description: Salem Arts Association is a nonprofit community arts organization located in Salem’s historic waterfront, nestled among the shops and restaurants of Pickering Wharf. Our galleries and gift shop offer affordable locally created fine art, crafts, jewelry, photography, unique gifts and more. We host regular exhibitions, workshops, and community events that celebrate local talent and foster creative community engagement, ensuring that everyone has an opportunity to enjoy our vibrant local arts scene.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 2007, we serve as an inclusive cultural hub for artists and art enthusiasts alike.

pois.short_description: Eliot Community Human Services provides a comprehensive network of rehabilitative services to adults, children, and families with complex needs, including mental health, behavioral health, and human services.
pois.long_description: Eliot Community Human Services is committed to serving vulnerable populations with limited resources. They offer a wide range of services, including behavioral health clinics, emergency services, substance use recovery programs, and support for individuals experiencing homelessness. Eliot collaborates with public and private partners to provide comprehensive, coordinated care.

pois.short_description: Salem t-shirt shop offering unique and spirited designs celebrating the town's magic for over 30 years.
pois.long_description: For over three decades, Witch Tee's has been Salem's go-to spot for t-shirts, sweatshirts, and more that are as unique and spirited as the town itself. They offer over 40 original designs blending Salem's mystique with humor, from cheeky slogans to adventure-ready themes. The shop is a traditional stop for visitors from around the world, offering a curated selection of conversation starters and cherished souvenirs.
pois.origin_story: Witch Tee's was founded over 30 years ago by Joan, a Salem native, who had a passion for collecting t-shirts and the idea to create a place where everyone could find high-quality, affordably priced tees celebrating Salem's magic.

pois.short_description: Historic bed and breakfast in downtown Salem, Massachusetts, offering beautifully renovated rooms and gourmet breakfast.
pois.long_description: The Amelia Payson House is a beautifully renovated historic building located in historic downtown Salem, MA. It features well-appointed guest rooms with gas fireplaces and Tempur-Pedic mattresses, offering a blend of vintage charm and modern comfort. Guests can enjoy gourmet breakfast, afternoon tea, and complimentary amenities like high-speed internet and on-site parking.
pois.origin_story: The Amelia Payson House was built in 1845 by Edward Payson for his wife Amelia. Edward H. Payson was born in Roxbury and began his business career in Salem, eventually becoming a cashier at the Commercial bank. They built their home at 16 Winter Street, now known as the Amelia-Payson House.

pois.short_description: The Peabody Essex Museum's photography collection spans three centuries, showcasing diverse techniques and global perspectives.
pois.long_description: PEM’s photography collection encourages different ways of seeing the world, encompassing beauty, records, scientific experiments, and remembrances. Reflecting the museum’s early focus on global exploration, the collection dates to 1855 and features works from East, South, and Southeast Asia, as well as contemporary photography. It highlights the role of photography in shaping visual cultures worldwide.
pois.origin_story: The collection dates to 1855 and reflects the global activities and spirit of the museum’s earliest supporters as voyagers who sought to explore and share the world with their communities.

pois.short_description: Magickal shop in Salem, MA offering handcrafted oils, herbs, crystals, jewelry, and hosting classes and readings.
pois.long_description: Enchanted is a magickal shop for Witches and Muggles alike, located in Salem, MA. The shop features warm colors, a friendly atmosphere, and a unique “Enchanted” tree. It offers a wide selection of handcrafted magickal items, including oils, herbs, stones, crystals, jewelry, soaps, teas, and more, created by local crafters and Salem Witches. Enchanted also provides classes, workshops, Reiki, and psychic/tarot readings.

pois.short_description: Explore lifelike exhibits, cobblestone streets & hands-on fun at Salem’s wax museum, where the past and Halloween meet.
Automotive repair shop serving Salem and neighboring towns for over 40 years, specializing in domestic and foreign repairs.
pois.long_description: Santisi’s Garage is the most trusted automotive mechanic repair shop in Salem, MA. We specialize in domestic and foreign repairs with a fast turnaround, at unbeatable quality. We have hundreds of returning customers year after year and prioritize our customer’s best interests.
pois.origin_story: Santisi’s Garage has been serving Salem and its neighboring towns for over 40 years.

pois.short_description: Modern take on popular classics surrounded by oak paneling and a wood-burning fireplace, with a full bar and views of Salem Common.
Catholic high school in Peabody, Massachusetts, focused on faith, integrity, relationship, scholarship, and tradition.
pois.long_description: Bishop Fenwick High School builds on the foundation established by families, preparing students to excel in college and career. The school emphasizes faith, integrity, relationship, scholarship, and tradition, fostering a positive learning environment with high expectations and strong supports. It offers a rich tradition of athletics, arts, and community involvement.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1959 by Richard Cardinal Cushing and staffed by the sisters of Notre Dame de Namur.

pois.short_description: The Coast Guard Aviation Association supports Coast Guard Aviation personnel and preserves its history.
pois.long_description: The Coast Guard Aviation Association (CGAA) is dedicated to supporting the men, women, and families of Coast Guard Aviation. It emphasizes recognizing individuals for operational missions and preserving Coast Guard Aviation history. The organization also provides support to families affected by flight operation losses.
pois.origin_story: The organization began as the Ancient Order of the Pterodactyl in 1978, initially supporting the Coast Guard exhibit at the Naval Air Museum.

pois.display_name: The Art Corner Custom Picture Frame Shop
pois.short_description: Custom picture framing since 1977, specializing in expert design and friendly service.
Online shop for the Peabody Essex Museum offering books, gifts, apparel, and accessories.
Stylish mid-century hotel in the heart of Salem offering modern accommodations.
Bed & breakfast steps away from Salem Harbor, The House of the Seven Gables, offering private baths, parking, hot breakfast, and a roof deck with ocean views.
pois.long_description: Morning Glory Bed & Breakfast is a beautifully restored Georgian Federal home, circa 1808, located in historic Salem MA. The inn is ideally situated on a very quiet, dead-end street, a stone’s throw away from beautiful Salem Harbor, across the street from the historic House of the Seven Gables, and an easy walk to all of the seaside restaurants, museums, and attractions Salem has to offer. The inn consistently ranks #1 among Salem’s Bed & Breakfast Inns.
pois.origin_story: Bob Shea, a native of Salem, dreamed of owning and operating a bed & breakfast after earning a degree in Marine engineering and sailing as a merchant marine.

pois.short_description: Upbeat American tavern opposite the Peabody-Essex Museum, known for its burgers, wraps, pizza, wings, and wide selection of beers.
pois.long_description: Village Tavern is an upbeat American tavern located opposite the Peabody-Essex Museum. It has been voted “Salem’s Number One Burger” for eight years in a row. The tavern offers amazing wraps, hand-tossed pizza, wings, and tasty appetizers, complemented by 75 varieties of domestic and craft beers, making it a perfect spot for lunch or dinner in Salem.

pois.short_description: Auto parts store offering a wide range of automotive parts, accessories, and services.
pois.long_description: AutoZone Auto Parts Salem #5075 is a one-stop shop for top-quality auto parts, accessories, and trustworthy advice to keep your car, truck, or SUV running smoothly. They offer free in-store battery testing, charging, and recycling, as well as a Fix Finder service to diagnose vehicle issues and a Loan-A-Tool program for DIY repairs.

pois.short_description: Georgian mansion and garden in Salem, home to three generations of the Ropes family and now a historic house museum.
pois.long_description: Ropes Mansion (1727–1729) was built in the Georgian style and renovated in 1894 in the Colonial Revival style. The stately mansion was home to three generations of the Ropes family and is recognized as one of New England's most significant and thoroughly documented historic houses. Filled with original furnishings, the house contains superb examples of 18th and 19th-century furniture, ceramics, silver, and textiles. The tranquil Ropes Mansion Garden was designed in 1912 and is open to the public 365 days a year.
pois.origin_story: In 1893, three unmarried Ropes sisters moved to Salem after inheriting the mansion and a fortune, with plans to transform it into a comfortable home and Salem’s first historic house museum.

pois.short_description: Coffee shop in Salem offering coffee, breakfast, and sandwiches, with early morning hours to serve commuters.
Salem shop offering apparel, accessories, cosmetics, ritual tools, gifts, and more.
Salem brewery founded in 2014 by two local friends, offering small-batch craft beer and constantly iterating on styles.
pois.long_description: East Regiment Beer Co. is a Salem brewery started in 2014 by Scott Perry and Joshua Engdahl. The brewery is located in the Original Fire House, a 170-year-old building that required extensive renovation. The owners hand-built a nano brewery system, including the brew stove, kettles, and fermenters. In 2024, Joshua Engdahl departed, leaving Scott Perry at the helm, focused on constantly improving beer styles through rapid iteration and experimentation.
pois.origin_story: East Regiment was started in 2014 by Scott Perry and Joshua Engdahl, two Salem locals and longtime friends. They were avid homebrewers who decided to start a brewery in their hometown after success in competitions. They acquired the Original Fire House in Salem in 2016.

pois.short_description: Walking photoshoots visiting Salem's famous sites, including Hocus Pocus locations, the Witch House, and Salem Common.
Salem walking tours led by passionate guides, offering gripping tales and surprising truths about the Witch Trials and the city's dark history.
pois.long_description: Salem Uncovered Tours offers 90-minute walking tours that delve into the real story of Salem, going beyond clichés to uncover gripping tales and surprising truths. They focus on the human stories behind historical events, emphasizing loss, grief, success, and the complexities of the past. The tours explore the Salem Witch Trials, dark mysteries, murder, and the macabre, with a focus on historical accuracy and passionate storytelling.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 2020 as Better Than Fiction Tours, the company rebranded as Salem Uncovered Tours in 2024. It originated from the restrictions and opportunities presented by the COVID-19 pandemic, embracing the power of storytelling to connect with the past.

pois.short_description: Dual specialists in pediatric dentistry and orthodontics offering comprehensive, gentle dental and orthodontic care for children, teens, and adults.
pois.long_description: At Joie Smiles, they believe every child deserves a beautiful, healthy start, and every adult deserves the confidence that comes with a perfect smile. They offer seamless, specialized care in both Pediatric Dentistry and Orthodontics—all under one roof. Their clinics are designed to be fun, friendly, and stress-free for the entire family, providing the highest standard of care from childhood through adulthood.

pois.short_description: Cafe featuring specialty drinks and treats inspired by the Witch City, built to resemble a witch’s cottage.
Salem’s original and best paranormal tour offering ghost tours focused on history, hauntings, voodoo, and vampirism.
pois.long_description: Spellbound Tours offers nightly tours covering the history of Salem’s most haunted places and their own experiences investigating them. Tours visit actual locations from the infamous witch trials, explore voodoo roots, and delve into tales of vampirism. Guests are taught scientific methods of ghost detection and encouraged to capture paranormal energy with their cameras. Spellbound Tours prides itself on historical accuracy and professionalism, led by licensed and experienced paranormal investigators.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1999 by a professional parapsychologist and investigator, Spellbound Tours established itself as a pioneer in ghost hunting before it became popular. The business was established in 2000 and 2001 according to different sources.

pois.short_description: Historic hotel in downtown Salem, MA offering 93 rooms and a blend of elegance and comfort.
pois.long_description: The Hawthorne Hotel is a historic gem nestled in the heart of downtown Salem, MA. As one of the top-rated Salem MA hotels, our 93-room property invites you to step into a world where elegance meets comfort. The Hawthorne’s grand façade opens into a welcoming space filled with rich history and classic New England style. Whether you’re visiting for a peaceful getaway, a vibrant wedding, or a memorable special event, the Hawthorne Hotel is the perfect starting point to explore everything this remarkable city has to offer.
pois.origin_story: Established in 1925, the Hawthorne Hotel has a long history and passion for hosting weddings.

pois.short_description: Open-air waterfront bar in Salem offering cold drinks, live music, and events.
pois.long_description: The Cannery is an open-air bar tucked along the Beverly-Salem harbor, inviting visitors to slow down and enjoy sea air, music, and sunsets. It aims to capture the spirit of old waterfront packing houses—functional, a little rough around the edges, and built for people who enjoy a good time.
pois.origin_story: The Cannery is named after the old waterfront packing houses and the people who worked them.
pois.street_address: 2 Bridge St Rear, Salem, MA 01970, USA

pois.short_description: One of the oldest Protestant churches in North America, founded in 1629, offering religious fellowship and spiritual exploration in a Unitarian Universalist tradition.
pois.long_description: The First Church in Salem is a historic Unitarian Universalist congregation founded in 1629, making it one of the oldest Protestant churches in North America. It welcomes individuals of diverse theological and philosophical backgrounds, emphasizing values of freedom, reason, and justice. The church offers religious education for all ages, community programming, and hosts events like tours and workshops. It has a complex history of separations and mergers with other congregations in Salem, and is deeply connected to the city's landmarks.
pois.origin_story: Gathered by the Puritans of Massachusetts Bay Colony in August of 1629, the First Church in Salem is one of the oldest Protestant churches founded in North America and the first to be governed by congregational polity.

pois.short_description: Salem’s oldest established professional independent walking tour company offering historical and paranormal tours since 1997.
pois.long_description: Salem Historical Tours is a family-owned and operated company providing a wide range of both historical and paranormal tours, covering Salem's 400 years of history. They offer guided experiences led by knowledgeable and charismatic guides, focusing on Salem’s macabre past, documented paranormal activity, and authentic haunted history.
pois.origin_story: Established in 1997, Salem Historical Tours has been the Witch City's oldest established professional independent walking tour company for over 28 years.

pois.short_description: Wholesale and retail supplier of plumbing, heating, and cooling products.
Salon offering color applications and precision cutting skills in a low-key atmosphere.
pois.long_description: Bethany Taryn Amos began her career with an intensive apprenticeship at a Newbury Street salon, gaining experience in Boston and New York while continuing her training at national hair academies. After returning to the North Shore and experiencing high demand, she founded Bethany Taryn Salon. Stylists are trained in innovative techniques and specialize in flawless color and precision cuts, offering top-notch services in a low-key atmosphere.
pois.origin_story: Bethany Taryn Amos founded the salon after experiencing high business volume and needing to expand her services.

pois.short_description: Modern boutique hotel in Salem, blending history and hospitality with themed rooms inspired by Salem’s iconic past.
pois.long_description: The Cove at Salem is a modern boutique hotel immersed in Salem’s rich local culture. The 57-room hotel is a captivating blend of history & hospitality, and features themed-rooms inspired by Salem’s iconic past.

pois.short_description: Food pantry operating a storefront in downtown Salem and mobile pop-up pantries across the North Shore.
pois.long_description: The Salem Pantry operates both a storefront in downtown Salem and mobile pop-up pantries across the North Shore — including in Lynn, Swampscott, and Beverly — to meet families where they are. They are preparing for a potential surge in need due to uncertainty around SNAP benefits and are urging residents to stay current on their paperwork.

pois.short_description: New England-inspired apparel and accessories, including t-shirts, flannels, jeans, and home goods.
pois.long_description: District Trading Company creates apparel and accessories inspired by New England's coastal towns, sandy shores, and creative culture. They offer a range of products including t-shirts, tanks, sweatshirts, hoodies, long sleeves, hats, accessories, jewelry, home goods, cards, prints, and stickers.

pois.display_name: The Dotty Brown Art & Nature Center
pois.short_description: Family-friendly center at the Peabody Essex Museum showcasing connections with nature through contemporary art, interactive experiences, and hands-on displays.
pois.long_description: The Dotty Brown Art & Nature Center showcases original exhibitions that investigate our connections with nature through contemporary art, memorable objects and interactive experiences. This family-friendly center features changing exhibitions, hands-on displays and art making spaces, encouraging visitors to touch, play, and explore.

pois.short_description: Salem Haunted Tours offers approximately 90-minute walking tours of Salem's history, legends, and haunted sites, led by local licensed guides.
pois.long_description: Salem Haunted Tours provides a genuine, unscripted experience led by a true local who knows the history, legends, and mysteries of Salem firsthand. The tours dive deep into the history of Salem, covering indigenous people, first encounters, witch trials, seafaring exploits, military skirmishes, haunted sites, and modern-day pop culture film sites. Tours begin in the maritime district and end in downtown Salem near shops and restaurants.

pois.display_name: James B. and Mary Lou Hawkes Collection Center
pois.short_description: State-of-the-art facility in Rowley, Massachusetts for the preservation, research, and storage of PEM’s collection.
pois.long_description: The James B. and Mary Lou Hawkes Collection Center is a 120,000-square-foot facility in Rowley, Massachusetts where PEM is engaged in a multi-year project to move its entire collection. It’s where conservation, cleaning, pest protection, photography, documentation and research take place, unifying, preserving and protecting over 850,000 works of art and culture. The Collection Center also houses the entirety of PEM’s Phillips Library, with several hundred thousand books and over a linear mile of archival material.
pois.origin_story: The Collection Center received a generous donation from James B. and Mary Lou Hawkes in 2023 to support efforts to house, care for, and improve access to the collection.

pois.short_description: Boating option on Massachusetts’ North Shore since 1934, offering boating, banquets, parties, and family events.
pois.origin_story: Established in 1934, Palmer’s Cove Yacht Club has been a boating option on Massachusetts’ North Shore.

pois.short_description: Artisan bakery & cafe offering breads, pastries, soups & sandwiches made from local ingredients.
Modern European restaurant in downtown Salem featuring fresh fish, seasonal vegetables, and homemade pastas.
Enchanting retail haven offering one-of-a-kind gifts and artisanal treasures, including signature witch brooms and DIY kits.
pois.long_description: Step into our brand new enchanting retail haven, where magic meets artistry! Our curated collection features one-of-a-kind gifts and artisanal treasures from local and regional artisans. Discover our signature witch brooms and DIY broom kits, meticulously handcrafted to bring a piece of Salem home. Our shelves are adorned with an array of delights, including handcrafted candles, stylish apparel, books & stationery, and seasonal decor.

pois.short_description: The official website for the Town of Andover, Massachusetts, providing access to news, events, government services, and frequently asked questions.
Audio-guided journey separating witchcraft myths from reality. Shop Pagan & Wiccan items made by local witches.
Replica of the 1812 privateer FAME offering sails from Pickering Wharf, with hands-on adventure, cannon fire, and maritime history lessons.
pois.long_description: Sail on the top-rated boat tour in Salem! Our replica of the 1812 privateer FAME sails 3-4 times daily from Pickering Wharf, right downtown among the shops and restaurants. A hands-on adventure where you can raise the sails, hear the roar of the cannon, and learn about Salem’s epic maritime history! A great trip for families and kids, provided they are at least five years old. Drinks and snacks (including beer & wine) sold on board. Book in advance for our popular sunset trips! You haven’t seen Salem until you’ve seen it from the water.

pois.short_description: Family-owned and operated since 1933, Gardner Mattress handcrafts mattresses in New England using high-quality, responsibly sourced materials.
pois.long_description: Gardner Mattress has distinguished itself in the mattress industry for maintaining a standard of high-grade craftsmanship, with a commitment to quality construction and reliable durability. They offer a range of collections, including the Natural Sleep Collection with organic options like Organic Vegan, Organic Classic, Organic Ergo, Organic Harmony, and Organic Ultra Luxe, as well as a Traditional Collection known for its family history and handcrafted quality.
pois.origin_story: Founded in 1933, Gardner Mattress began with a focus on natural materials, initially using horse hair. The company has maintained a commitment to handcrafted quality for almost a century.

pois.short_description: Specialty chocolate company offering authentic and historic drinking chocolate elixirs, truffles, caramels, and solid chocolates.

They offer a variety of drinking chocolate elixirs representing recipes from 1000 BC to the mid-1900s AD, including Pre-Columbian, Mesoamerican, Mayan, Aztec, European, and Colonial American/Mexican styles. Kakawa also creates unique truffles, caramels, and solid chocolates using fresh, seasonal ingredients, with options like Pomegranate, Cherry-Chili, and Goat Cheese & Sage.
pois.origin_story: The company was founded with a passion for authentic and historic drinking chocolates and has earned a reputation as one of the most exciting and original chocolate shops in the world.

Latino-owned businesses are a rapidly growing sector of the U.S. economy, driving job creation and economic growth. pois.origin_story: In 2020, Emily Mitchell started Hilltop Learning, offering reading support to students in a small outdoor classroom. The program moved indoors in 2021 and to its current location at 71 Atlantic Avenue in 2024.

pois.short_description: Orthodontic practice offering braces and Invisalign for children, teens, and adults in the North Shore area.
pois.long_description: At New England Orthodontic Specialists, we believe orthodontics is so much more than straight teeth. A new smile transforms your health, your looks and your confidence. From children to adults, we welcome your whole family to enjoy a healthier, more beautiful smile through professional orthodontics. We combine expertise with unique financing and treatment options, making it easier than ever to transform your smile.
